description:
Goal: make callable-boundary obligations explicit Compiler2 artifact facts. Signal: every callable value that survives to a closed callable boundary has a declared callable entry target, and unused callable syntax creates no backend obligation.

Change:
- Walk pruned materialized bodies/AbiReady executables to find closed FunctionRef, Lambda, and ClosureCall usage that requires a callable entry.
- Derive callable-entry obligations from closed use and settled types, not from raw syntax and not from old callable_entry_targets.
- Represent obligations with Compiler2 ids and Ty handles, aligned with ExecutableKey/ExecutableNeed where a concrete executable target is known.
- Fatal if a callable boundary needs an entry that cannot be resolved from closed facts.

Why:
- Callable entries are representation obligations created by surviving executable use. They are not semantic reachability discovery and they should not be selected by backend fallback code.

Checks:
- Enum.reduce proves the reducer callable entry is present and sufficient.
- Quicksort plus uncalled foo proves cold functions and unused callable values do not create entries.
- A focused negative test proves an unresolved opaque callable boundary is fatal.
- Telemetry emits existing ids/facts/opaque obligations only; no formatted planned-target strings.
title: Compiler2 artifacts: derive callable-entry obligations from closed use
